brewery.brewery_description:
Valley Hops Brewing crafts independent beer with character, for the true characters, at a rooftop brewery in the heart of Fortitude Valley.

Valley Hops Brewing is a rooftop brewery above Cloudland in the thumping heart of Brisbane’s Fortitude Valley. Here, you’ll find us brewing independent beer that’s crafted with character, for the true characters.

We love the Valley – that’s why we decided it deserved its own beer. And at Valley Hops Brewing, we’re celebrating this unique place in all its true colour.

Pull up a seat at our rooftop brewery, allow us to pour you a Valley-born brew and soak in the energy of the ever-changing neighbourhood we’re proud to call home.

This is the Valley, we’re its people, and this is its beer.

beer.beer_name: Nocturnal Animals - Fiesta Mango & Passionfruit Sour

beer.beer_description: This one’s a bit of a show-off. Our Exhibitionist Tropical Ale screams ‘look at me!’ with its lively fruit aromas and light, dry finish. This beer’s an enthusiastic nod to the musos, the artists, the fashionistas, the designers, the hipsters and all the out-of-the-boxers who strut their stuff on the Valley’s lively streets. Best paired with a spot of people watching, take a sip of the Exhibitionist and unleash your inner Valley eccentric.

items.badge_description: Your quest to quench your thirst leaves no stone unturned! That's at least 190 distinct venues!

beer.beer_description: From the misunderstood artist to the boundary-pushing entrepreneur, our Interloper Hazy IPA’s a beer for the misfits and the big-dreamers – for those who vibrate on a different frequency to the rest. Here in the Valley, everyone’s welcome to become part of the jumble of people that make this place unique. This distinctive beer with its malty fullness, silky mouthfeel and citrus aromas is a toast to the interlopers.

Peaking in the 1990s, this boozy fun run saw groups of mates pulling on their running shorts and fluro mesh singlets, clipping up their cash-filled bum bags and run a 16-kilometre course through inner Brisbane, stopping at 21 pubs along the way for a pot. As the story goes, runners were paired-up to make sure their run buddy didn’t cheat, both the run and the beer quota. The 21 Pubs Pale Ale is our cheers to these fine athletic specimens. A refreshing, medium-bodied and hard-working American pale ale.

items.badge_description: Your quest to quench your thirst leaves no stone unturned! That's at least 185 distinct venues!

Want to enjoy a day of brews, but need to keep your wits sharp? There are plenty of delicious brews under 5% for you to sip on throughout the day. That's 185 different beers with an ABV of 5% and lower. Try 5 more for Level 38!

beer.beer_description: Peaking in the 1990s, this boozy fun run saw groups of mates pulling on their running shorts and fluro mesh singlets, clipping up their cash-filled bum bags and run a 16-kilometre course through inner Brisbane, stopping at 21 pubs along the way for a pot. As the story goes, runners were paired-up to make sure their run buddy didn’t cheat, both the run and the beer quota. The 21 Pubs Pale Ale is our cheers to these fine athletic specimens. A refreshing, medium-bodied and hard-working American pale ale.

Spice up your life with Bullet Tooth whenever you wanna zigazig ah over ‘zingiber officinale.’ Best served with a wedge of lime, this is a ginger beer with bite, slightly sweet flavour and dry finish.

items.badge_description: Want to enjoy a day of brews, but need to keep your wits sharp? There are plenty of delicious brews under 5% for you to sip on throughout the day. That's 275 different beers with an ABV of 5% and lower.

Fresh from the tap is one of the best ways to enjoy a beer. Just try to use a glass and not drink directly from the tap, as tempting as it may be. That's 475 checkins with the serving style of Draft. Try 5 more for Level 96!

beer.beer_description: Introducing the Traditional Ale that’s as smooth as any diamond geezer you’ll find outside the pub with a pint in his hand on game day. Served on nitro, our Geezer’s a loveable rogue with a soft, creamy head that lingers. He’ll welcome you with biscuits, honey, a nutty undertone and a whiff of citrus, with a rich caramel flavour—like a bloke with a sweet tooth and a sharp wit. Let this copper-hued charmer win you over with every smooth and satisfying sip. Geezer represents all of the hard working, friendly folk from the Valley, past and present. You know their names. We’ve got plenty of juicy stories, but we’ll never talk.

items.badge_description: Your quest to quench your thirst leaves no stone unturned! That's at least 175 distinct venues!

Round and round the wheel goes! Which style you’ll be drinking, nobody knows! Your desire to explore different styles is inspiring. Keep it up! That's a check-in to at least 160 different styles. Try 5 more for Level 33!

items.checkin_comment: Smooth with the Nitro and serving temp was pretty good too. Smooth malty roasty coffee.

user.bio:
Craft Beer is never gonna give you up, 
never gonna let you down.
UT Moderator, Craft beer nut. Love the hops, love the darks, love the travelling and hanging out with beery people!

travelling the oceans to find and save the finest (wh)ales
I prefer my kale with a silent „k“
No friends who don't comment on check in, sorry but checks in without comment are boring. I'm here for the craic.
Drinky drinky, time for less thinky.

items.checkin_comment: A more decent IPA then I was expecting to be honest. Citrus and some grassy Motueka in the mix.

user.bio:
Craft Beer is never gonna give you up, 
never gonna let you down.

beer.beer_description: A vibrant IPA crafted with an unconventional no-boil technique. This brew showcases the unique Pomona yeast, which imparts a distinct tropical fruit complexity. The hop profile: Citra- zesty citrus and grapefruit, Rakau - stone fruit sweetness, and Motueka- lime notes. The result is a smooth, juicy, IPA.

I like beer, all beer. Hoppy is good.  Very hoppy is excellent.  Fruit or sour is bad :-(

With a full-bodied flavour that takes centre stage, this Premium Lager balances smooth, malty sweetness with a bold, unapologetic bitterness.

items.comment: Enjoying the sweet cold nectar of Fortitude Valley I see

beer.beer_description:
A beer for the champs, the sloggers, and the battlers. Diehards Original Lager salutes our true-blue local Rugby League team — Valleys Diehards. Just like its namesake, this full-strength beer gives 110 percent. They say when you join the Diehards family, it becomes part of your DNA — in the same way this lager recipe has become part of ours. This is a crisp, easy-drinking, ‘proper lager’ that won’t let the team down.

Malt: Pale Malt and Flaked Rice

Hops: Topaz

items.badge_description: There's beer in them thar hills! You're setting off on your journey through the world of beer, but be sure to take a little time to get to know each brewery as you go. That's a beer from 275 different breweries. Try 5 more for Level 56!

Fresh from the tap is one of the best ways to enjoy a beer. Just try to use a glass and not drink directly from the tap, as tempting as it may be. That's 455 checkins with the serving style of Draft. Try 5 more for Level 92!
After a long day, what better way to kick back than with a crisp and refreshing lager? You're already feeling more relaxed, aren’t you?. That’s 115 different Lagers! Try 5 more for Level 24!

items.badge_name: Beyond a Shadow of a Stout (Level 18)
items.badge_description: We all love Stouts, and now we have a dedicated badge to celebrate your dedication to these dark, top-fermented beer in multiple variations, like Oatmeal, Milk and more! Which one will you start with? That's 90 different beers with the style of Stout. Try 5 more for Level 19!

beer.beer_description: A vibrant IPA crafted with an unconventional no-boil technique. This brew showcases the unique Pomona yeast, which imparts a distinct tropical fruit complexity. The hop profile: Citra- zesty citrus and grapefruit, Rakau - stone fruit sweetness, and Motueka- lime notes. The result is a smooth, juicy, IPA.

items.badge_description: Porters and Stouts are rich with both history and flavor. Roasty, dark, and delicious, these two styles helped start it all. That’s 125 different beers with the style of Porter or Stout. Try 5 more for Level 26!

Everyone loves fresh beer, but obviously not as much as you do. You went straight to the source. 

That's a beer at 25 different venues categorized as a Brewery, Cidery or Meadery. Visit 5 more for Level 6!

Here in the Valley, everyone’s welcome to become part of the jumble of people that make this place unique. This distinctive beer with its malty fullness, silky mouthfeel and citrus aromas is a toast to the interlopers.

This one’s a bit of a show-off. Our Exhibitionist Tropical Ale screams ‘look at me!’ with its lively fruit aromas and light, dry finish. This beer’s an enthusiastic nod to the musos, the artists, the fashionistas, the designers, the hipsters and all the out-of-the-boxers who strut their stuff on the Valley’s lively streets. Best paired with a spot of people watching, take a sip of the Exhibitionist and unleash your inner Valley eccentric.

As the story goes, runners were paired-up to make sure their run buddy didn’t cheat, both the run and the beer quota. The 21 Pubs Pale Ale is our cheers to these fine athletic specimens. beer.beer_name: Nocturnal Animals - Fiesta Mango & Passionfruit Sour
beer.beer_description: Amps on, cue the live music and kick things off with a slightly sweet and very ‘Queensland’ sour that boasts all the liveliness of the Valley Fiesta. Our refreshing ode to good times in the Valley will soon have you dancing down Brunswick Street Mall. Bursting with summer fruits, our Fiesta Mango & Passionfruit Sour is loud, unforgettable and celebrates local talent – just like all good music festivals should.

A beer for the champs, the sloggers, and the battlers. Diehards Original Lager salutes our true-blue local Rugby League team — Valleys Diehards. Just like its namesake, this full-strength beer gives 110 percent. They say when you join the Diehards family, it becomes part of your DNA — in the same way this lager recipe has become part of ours. This is a crisp, easy-drinking, ‘proper lager’ that won’t let the team down.

Malt: Pale Malt and Flaked Rice

Hops: Topaz

Take only what you need. Leave no trace. This guy’s a true nature-lover, tuned to feel your vibrations and inner presence, with an impressive web of knowledge at his rather hairy fingertips. Be the yin to his yang with a Herbalist IPA and thank the wise ‘Father’ of Chinatown for bringing his ancient wisdom to the Valley. You’ll be rewarded with a toffee-like malt profile, pine and citrus aromas, and a bittersweet finish.
Clean and super crisp cold IPA with bold watermelon flavours backed by fresh tropical notes.
100% fermented with wild yeast, this Aussie IPA is surprisingly clean while boasting ripe pineapple, peach and citrus with just the right amount of funk.

beer.beer_name: Nocturnal Animals - Widows Weeds Oatmeal Stout
beer.beer_description: Rich, velvety mouthfeel with flavors and aromas of toffee, chocolate and roasted coffee beans.

Lazy Bones Extra Dry IPA is the perfect brew for those who’ve mastered the art of doing nothing. With a casual blend of ripe peach, subtle lime, and citrus notes, it’s like a dreamy peach Bellini—for people who’d rather lift a pint than a finger.

Introducing the Traditional Ale that’s as smooth as any diamond geezer you’ll find outside the pub with a pint in his hand on game day. Served on nitro, our Geezer’s a loveable rogue with a soft, creamy head that lingers. He’ll welcome you with biscuits, honey, a nutty undertone and a whiff of citrus, with a rich caramel flavour—like a bloke with a sweet tooth and a sharp wit. Let this copper-hued charmer win you over with every smooth and satisfying sip. Geezer represents all of the hard working, friendly folk from the Valley, past and present. You know their names. We’ve got plenty of juicy stories, but we’ll never talk.

A stayer, and a true-blue team player.

When you’re in need of thirst-quenching refreshment that goes the distance, Diehards Dummy Half delivers the goods. Every. Single. Time.

Our trusty, local Rugby League team knows a well-balanced and reliable player stays on top of their game, until the final whistle blows. Likewise, we know you’ll want to keep this seriously sessionable mid-strength on side, for long days spent playing in our glorious Sunshine State.

Pace yourself with this crisp and refreshing lager anytime, anywhere you need true staying power.
Easy drinking Pilsner. Firm bitterness, full bodied, well rounded. Sweet malty flavour of fresh bread crust and biccies.
Dark rice lager.

Belgian style witbier on nitro with raspberry and hibiscus. Tartness from the Belgian yeast, raspberry and hibiscus balanced out by the soft creamy texture of the nitro pour along with it’s usual besties of orange zest and coriander seed along for this pink zippy ride.

Spice up your life with Bullet Tooth whenever you wanna zigazig ah over ‘zingiber officinale.’ Best served with a wedge of lime, this is a ginger beer with bite, slightly sweet flavour and dry finish.
Oat Cream IPA
A good, rich, flavoursome, fruity IPA.

A vibrant IPA crafted with an unconventional no-boil technique. This brew showcases the unique Pomona yeast, which imparts a distinct tropical fruit complexity. The hop profile: Citra- zesty citrus and grapefruit, Rakau - stone fruit sweetness, and Motueka- lime notes. The result is a smooth, juicy, IPA.

Save the planet and show some love for this Brisbane Brewer’s Club collab, made with recycled hops and yeast. Home brewers may be thrifty, but this XPA is rich with the fruity notes of passionfruit, mandarin and naval orange.
With a full-bodied flavour that takes centre stage, this Premium Lager balances smooth, malty sweetness with a bold, unapologetic bitterness.

A heavenly indulgence with aromas and flavours of banoffee pie that will leave you in a state of pure bliss. Snowy’s Serenade is the perfect blend of sweet and dry, with a warm finish and rocky head that’s sure to make you melt.
